zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Clare於1287年4月1日出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 克萊爾布拉迪 卡素時，她的父親Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond42歲，母親Lady Juliane FitzMaurice25歲。她於1303年之前與Gilbert De Umfreville結婚。她去世於大約1333年在英國倫敦享年47歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Clare was born on 1 April 1287, in Bunratty Castle, County Clare, Ireland, her father, Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond, was 42 and her mother, Lady Juliane FitzMaurice, was 25. She married Gilbert De Umfreville before 1303. She died about 1333, in London, England, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Clare est née le 1 avril 1287, à Bunratty Castle, County Clare, Irlande, son père, Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Lady Juliane FitzMaurice, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Gilbert De Umfreville avant 1303. Elle est décédée environ 1333, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Clare am 1. April 1287 in Bunratty Castle, County Clare, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond 42 und ihre Mutter Lady Juliane FitzMaurice 25. Sie hat vor 1303 Gilbert De Umfreville geheiratet. Sie starb um 1333 im Alter von 47 Jahren in London, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Clare è nata il il 1 aprile 1287 nel Bunratty Castle, County Clare, Irlanda, sua padre, Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Lady Juliane FitzMaurice, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Gilbert De Umfreville prima del 1303. Morì circa 1333, nel London, England, all'età di 47 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Clareが1287年4月1日にアイルランドコウントイ クラーブンラッティ カストルで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomondは42歳，母Lady Juliane FitzMauriceは25歳であった。1303年以前にGilbert De Umfrevilleと結婚した。彼女は約1333年にイギリスロンドンで47歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Clare(이)가 1287년 4월 1일 아일랜드 카운티 클래르 버래티 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Juliane FitzMaurice(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1303년 이전 Gilbert De Umfreville(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 대략 1333년 영국 런던에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.Between October 1293 and April 1342: de outubro de 1293 a abril de 1342
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Clare nasceu em 1 de abril de 1287, em Bunratty Castle, County Clare, Irlanda, seu pai, Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Lady Juliane FitzMaurice, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Gilbert De Umfreville antes 1303. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1333, em London, Inglaterra, com 47 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Clare родилась 1 апреля 1287 г. в местности бунратти кастл, коунти клар, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Juliane FitzMaurice, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gilbert De Umfreville перед 1303 г. Она умерла около 1333 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 47 лет.
